platform.name: Flutter
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: Flutter's quick start does not require a Google, Flutter, or Dart developer account.
The representative path uses the quick start's default Windows configuration unless another OS is selected on the page.
Git, VS Code, and Flutter are not yet configured.

developer_goal: Install Flutter with VS Code, create the sample application, run it on Chrome, and verify stateful hot reload.

first_success.outcome: The generated counter app runs in Chrome and applies a stateful hot-reload change from increment to decrement.
first_success.observable_signal: After incrementing, changing _counter++ to _counter--, and hot reloading, the existing value remains and the next click decreases it.

route_selection.surface: Flutter docs Quick start: Set up and test drive Flutter
route_selection.rule: Prefer the vendor Quick start over custom setup; keep the page's default OS path as the representative candidate.
route_selection.selected: Set up and test drive Flutter (VS Code quick start)

candidates.name: Quick start with VS Code
candidates.reason: Labeled Quick start and ends with create, run on web, and try stateful hot reload.

candidates.name: Custom Flutter setup
candidates.reason: Intended for other editors or manual setup; points VS Code users to quick start.

candidates.name: Flutter learning pathway / alternate codelabs
candidates.reason: Peer learning routes also create and run a first app; consequential tie versus quick-start hot-reload terminal needs policy confirmation.

required_path.action: Install Git and Visual Studio Code for the selected OS, then install the Flutter VS Code extension.
required_path.observable_result: VS Code exposes Flutter commands in the Command Palette.

required_path.action: Use Flutter: New Project, Download SDK, clone Flutter, and add the SDK to PATH.
required_path.observable_result: The Flutter SDK is cloned and available on PATH after terminals/VS Code restart.

required_path.action: Create an Application project named trying_flutter (or another lowercase_with_underscores name).
required_path.observable_result: VS Code finishes Flutter project initialization and opens the app.

required_path.action: Select Chrome as the device and start debugging to run the app on the web.
required_path.observable_result: Chrome opens the running counter app.

required_path.action: Increment the counter, change _counter++ to _counter-- in lib/main.dart, save or Hot Reload, then click again.
required_path.observable_result: State is preserved and the next click decreases the value.

prerequisites.description: A supported development machine for the OS selected on the quick start page.
Permission to install Git, VS Code, and clone the Flutter SDK.

unavoidable_waits.description: Flutter SDK clone and project dependency initialization.
First web build before Chrome shows the app.

platform_outcomes.description: Flutter tooling scaffolds the Application counter app and serves it to Chrome.
Hot reload applies the edited decrement behavior without restarting the app.

excluded.reason: Presented as optional during SDK setup.

excluded.item: Custom setup for non-VS Code editors
excluded.reason: Alternate surface; quick start is recommended for VS Code users.

excluded.item: Additional native target platform tooling beyond Chrome web
excluded.reason: Quick start verifies on web first.

sources.title: Set up and test drive Flutter
Custom Flutter setup
evidence_supported: Custom setup as alternate to quick start

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus account-creation starting boundary map for Flutter, which has no vendor account on the quick start?
uncertainties.impact: Prevents verified status under the account-creation contract.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Corpus policy for open-source frameworks without platform accounts.

uncertainties.question: Which OS path is the canonical measured route when the quick start page branches across Windows, macOS, Linux, and ChromeOS?
uncertainties.impact: Prerequisite package and Git install steps differ by OS and change action counts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Explicit selection of one OS representative path for corpus measurement.
